name: Brandied Chocolate Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring whipping cream and unsalted butter to a simmer in a heavy medium saucepan.
2. Reduce heat to low. Add 1/2 pound bittersweet chocolate and stir until melted.
3. Mix in 2 tablespoons Cognac. Let stand at room temperature until firm, about 3-1/2 hours.
4. Line a cookie sheet with foil. Spoon truffle mixture by rounded tablespoons onto the prepared cookie sheet, spacing apart.
5. Chill until firm, about 1 hour.
6. Roll each truffle between palms of hands into a ball. Place on the same sheet.
7. Repeat with remaining truffles; refrigerate.
8. Melt 3/4 pound chocolate in the top of a double boiler over simmering water, stirring frequently until smooth.
9. Remove from heat. Dip one truffle at a time into chocolate, coating completely.
10. Shake gently to remove excess chocolate. Place on the same sheet.
11. Repeat dipping with remaining truffles.
12. Refrigerate until firm, about 30 minutes.
13. Remove truffles from foil. (Can be prepared up to 1 week ahead. Refrigerate in an airtight container.)
14. Serve cold.
